compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps

1. Run the store-format symlink test: `pytest tests/interfaces/store/test_format_symlink_tracker.py::test_format_via_symlink_tracker -q` (observed failing on a macOS CI runner).
2. Observed in gerrit-verify run #28839737472 — 1 failed of 4570; sibling patchsets at the same commit passed, so it is intermittent / environment-sensitive, not a hard regression.

## Expected vs Actual

- Expected: `test_format_via_symlink_tracker[auto]` passes deterministically on every run and platform.
- Actual: intermittent `AssertionError: assert 35 <= 32` — the test asserts an upper bound on a count (a line/entry count in the formatted output rendered through a symlinked tracker) that is environment-sensitive and occasionally exceeds the hardcoded bound (32).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Root cause of the count variance identified (what makes it 35 vs <=32; e.g. extra lines from environment, ordering, or symlink resolution).
- [ ] Test made deterministic — stabilize the input or assert on the real invariant rather than a brittle magic-number upper bound.
- [ ] Test passes reliably across repeated runs and both param variants (`[auto]` and siblings).

file_impact.reason: flaky env-sensitive count assertion to stabilize

compiled_state.title: Flaky test_format_via_symlink_tracker[auto]: env-sensitive count assertion (assert 35 <= 32)
